name: Asparagus with Ginger-Orange Butter
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons minced fresh cilantro or parsley
1 teaspoon minced peeled fresh ginger root
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate 2 teaspoons orange zest (colored part of peel) into a small bowl. Squeeze the juice from the orange.
Add the butter, orange juice, cilantro, ginger, and 1/4 teaspoon each salt and pepper to the bowl. Using a portable mixer, beat until well blended. Wrap tightly in plastic wrap and roll up to form a 3-inch log, twisting the ends.
Freeze for 30 minutes or refrigerate overnight.
2. Once the butter mixture has set, cook the asparagus: Peel the bottom third of the asparagus spears using a vegetable peeler. In a large, deep skillet, combine 2 cups water, remaining orange juice, about 1/2 teaspoon salt, and the asparagus. Heat to boiling.
Reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, for 4 minutes or until tender. Drain and place on a platter.
Remove the butter from the freezer, unwrap it, and slice into 1/4-inch rounds. Place the slices on top of the hot asparagus.
Makes 6 servings.
Per serving: 120 calories, 5 grams protein, 6 grams carbohydrate, 10 grams fat, 26 milligrams cholesterol, 271 milligrams sodium.
